mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and force negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day SPX returns in over 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index below 250.0 signals disinflation that reduces real discount rates and triggers positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL drops below 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns across a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a high-inflation regime reflect capacity constraints that erode profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in high-inflation periods yield positive 5-day SPX returns in over 55 percent of cases.
